ggCowabunga: How do you know if something is too greedy
Notbiased: D19 was entirely to greedy

C[Senokir: I don't think greedy was the right word to describe D19 in my opinion, but generally speaking, a move is too greedy when you are try to take more than you deserve and ignore the moves you should play.  For instance, instead of D19 white really owed H9 to capture the stone in a ladder.  It doesn't matter if black ataris D19, the corner is still alive.  Capturing the J9 stone in a ladder makes white safe though which does matter.  So by playing D19 and ignoring the move that gives white safety you could consider it greedy in a way.  The reason I wouldn't describe it as greedy is because D19 is worth 1 point.  It seems more like a misread or a hallucination to me than being greedy.  Being greedy implies that white knew the risks of not capturing J9 and consciously ignored it in order to take a big move elsewhere.  D19 is not big though.
